scope: Safety information and Securite warnings draft batch for Sea Speak Linguist review. This is not an urgency or distress batch and must not be merged into starter.json before Director-Engineer acceptance, validator gate, QA regression, and integration.

qa_notes.linguist_note: Safety information is the branch meaning; security is ordinary English and must stay wrong.
Warning is kept separate from advice and information.
Danger is ordinary English here; draft trains hazard as the report term.
Visibility is not a substitute for weather or sea-state reports.
Wind is a weather element, not accepted as the word-item answer.
Obstacle is ordinary English but remains rejected here; Batch 004 trains obstruction as the strict report term.
Accept Securite/Sécurité only. Ordinary English security stays wrong.
Security warning is not accepted as a safety-signal substitute.
Navigation warning is distinct from weather warning and ordinary information.
Wind warning is rejected as not the trained phrase in this item.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: restricted visibility / poor visibility / visibility good
qa_notes.linguist_note: Matches existing strictness: poor/reduced visibility are not accepted substitutes.
Sea state is not a free synonym for weather or wind.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: listening watch / stand by / do not answer
qa_notes.linguist_note: Confirmed item-local variant: keep a listening watch is accepted. Keep watch is too broad.
Obstruction reported is a different report frame in this draft.
Obstacle reported remains rejected; obstruction reported is the trained safety report frame.
Read back is confirmation; say again asks for retransmission.

should_reject: pan pan pan pan pan pan
qa_notes.linguist_note: Safety signal only. Do not accept ordinary English security or other radio signals.

accepted_answers: securite navigation warning on channel one six
sécurité navigation warning on channel one six

should_accept: Securite, navigation warning on channel one six.
should_reject: security navigation warning on channel one six
securite weather warning on channel one six
securite navigation warning on channel one two
qa_notes.linguist_note: Channel number and warning type must stay exact.

Sea-state value must not be substituted.

Read back is confirmation and warning type stays exact.
Roger alone is not accepted; this trains explicit safety-information acknowledgement.
Advice is the noun; advise as a verb-form lookalike must stay wrong.

examples: Securite, Securite, Securite.
Securite, navigation warning on channel one six.
Securite, weather warning: strong wind from north.
Sea state is moderate.
Restricted visibility in the marina approach.
Obstruction near reporting point Alpha.
Hazard two cables north of the marina.
Navigation warning: obstruction in the channel.
Weather warning: sea state rough.
Keep listening watch on channel one six.
Read back navigation warning.
Safety information received.
Safety advice received.
Safety information valid until one four zero zero UTC.
Warning: obstruction east of the breakwater.
Visibility less than two nautical miles.

grammar_patterns.pattern: Securite three times + navigation warning + obstruction position
examples: Securite, Securite, Securite, navigation warning: obstruction two cables north of reporting point Alpha.

grammar_patterns.pattern: Securite + weather warning + wind + sea state
examples: Securite, weather warning: strong wind from north, sea state rough.

grammar_patterns.pattern: Navigation warning + restricted visibility + location + time
examples: Navigation warning: restricted visibility in the approach channel until one four zero zero UTC.
Hazard: bearing zero nine zero, distance two cables.

grammar_patterns.pattern: Read back safety warning + channel + time
examples: Read back safety warning, channel one six, one four zero zero UTC.
Correction, obstruction two cables east of the breakwater.
Keep listening watch until one five zero zero UTC.
Safety warning read back complete.

phrase_safety_securite_nav_obstruction_a: security security security navigation warning obstruction two cables north of reporting point alpha

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Securite/Sécurité is the safety signal; ordinary English security must stay wrong.

phrase_safety_securite_three_times_001: pan pan pan pan pan pan

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Safety signal must not collapse into other radio signal families.
Message category words carry different operational meaning.
Advice is a noun; advise is a verb-form lookalike and must not pass typo matching.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: restricted visibility / poor visibility / reduced visibility / visibility good

phrase_safety_restricted_visibility_mari: restricted visibility in the marina approach
poor visibility in the marina approach
reduced visibility in the marina approach
visibility good in the marina approach
phrase_safety_restricted_visibility_chan: navigation warning poor visibility in the approach channel until 1400 utc
navigation warning reduced visibility in the approach channel until 1400 utc

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Restricted visibility is the trained term and must not accept ordinary paraphrases or opposite visibility state.
Obstacle is ordinary English but Batch 004 trains obstruction as the safety-warning report term.

phrase_safety_navigation_warning_obstruc: weather warning obstruction in the channel

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Navigation and weather warnings are separate safety-information categories.
Hazard, obstruction, and ordinary danger wording are not interchangeable in this draft.

phrase_safety_weather_warning_wind_sea_0: securite weather warning visibility from north sea state rough

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Weather, wind, sea state, and visibility are related but distinct report elements.

phrase_safety_read_back_warning_channel_: read back safety warning channel 16 1400 utc
say again safety warning channel one six one four zero zero utc

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Read back confirms received information; say again asks for retransmission.

dangerous_minimal_pairs.pair: exact positions, channels, times, bearings, distances, directions

phrase_safety_hazard_two_cables_north_00: hazard 2 cables north of the marina
hazard two cables south of the marina
hazard two nautical miles north of the marina
phrase_safety_hazard_bearing_distance_00: hazard bearing zero nine zero distance two nautical miles

dangerous_minimal_pairs.reason: Safety-warning numerics and directions must not be fuzzed or loosely substituted.

producer_notes.note: Reviewed item-local variants: Sécurité with accent is accepted for Securite, channel 16 numeric and spoken forms are accepted item-locally, UTC/Zulu/Z forms are accepted item-locally, readback compact spelling is accepted for read-back items, keep a listening watch is accepted, and received safety information/advice word order is accepted when the safety term remains exact.

Confirmed rejected forms: ordinary English security for Securite/Sécurité, poor/reduced visibility for restricted visibility, danger for hazard, obstacle for obstruction, wind as a substitute for weather, and local time for UTC safety information.
Protect signal words, advisory nouns, channel numbers, UTC times, bearing 090 versus 90, cables versus nautical miles, and direction pairs north/south and east/west.
Batch 004 is safety information only. It contains no playable targets for other radio-signal families, rescue relay, legal vessel status, or assistance requests.
